questions.q: Rama Rajya refers to the reign of which king of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama Rajya is the legendary reign of Lord Rama, celebrated as the ideal kingdom.

questions.q: Over which kingdom did Rama rule during Rama Rajya?
questions.explanation: Rama ruled the kingdom of Ayodhya, the capital of Kosala.

questions.q: Rama Rajya is most commonly used as a byword for what kind of rule?
questions.explanation: Rama Rajya has become a byword for perfect, just, and prosperous governance.

questions.q: Who was the queen consort at the beginning of Rama's reign?
questions.explanation: Sita, Rama's devoted wife, was the queen consort of Ayodhya.

questions.q: Rama Rajya began immediately after which event?
options: The building of the bridge to Lanka
questions.explanation: Rama Rajya commenced after Rama was crowned king of Ayodhya following his exile.

questions.q: Which principle is said to have flourished during Rama Rajya?
questions.explanation: During Rama Rajya, dharma (righteousness) flourished throughout the kingdom.

questions.q: Rama Rajya is often described as a 'golden age' because of its widespread what?
questions.explanation: Rama Rajya is remembered as a golden age of universal prosperity and happiness.

questions.q: Who were the loyal brothers who supported Rama during his reign?
questions.explanation: Rama's brothers Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna loyally supported his rule.

questions.q: In which epic is the account of Rama Rajya found?
questions.explanation: The reign of Rama Rajya is described in the Ramayana, especially in the Uttara Kanda.

questions.q: During Rama Rajya, suffering among the people was said to have done what?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that during Rama Rajya all suffering ceased among the people.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of Valmiki's Ramayana is Rama Rajya primarily described?
questions.explanation: The reign of Rama Rajya is chiefly recounted in the Uttara Kanda, the final book.

questions.q: According to tradition, during Rama Rajya no parent had to do what?
options: Perform the funeral rites of a child
questions.explanation: It is said that during Rama Rajya no father ever had to perform the last rites of his son, as none died untimely.

questions.q: Which family priest (purohita) guided Rama during his reign?
questions.explanation: Sage Vasishtha was the royal preceptor and guide of the Ikshvaku dynasty during Rama's reign.

questions.q: The ideal of Rama Rajya was later invoked by which modern Indian leader as a vision of self-rule?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi frequently used 'Ram Rajya' as a symbol of an ideal, just society and self-rule.

questions.q: During Rama Rajya, the weather and rains were described as being what?
questions.explanation: Tradition says rains fell at the proper time and nature was bountiful, so crops never failed.

questions.q: Rama belonged to which royal dynasty whose ideals Rama Rajya embodied?
questions.explanation: Rama belonged to the Solar dynasty (Suryavamsha), descended from Ikshvaku.

questions.q: Which great horse sacrifice did Rama perform during his reign as a mark of sovereignty?
questions.explanation: Rama performed the Ashvamedha (horse sacrifice) during his reign, a rite asserting imperial sovereignty.

questions.q: During Rama's Ashvamedha, in place of a living queen beside him, what was used?
options: A statue or golden image of Sita
questions.explanation: Since Sita had departed to the forest, a golden image of Sita was placed beside Rama for the sacrifice.

questions.q: Who were the twin sons born to Sita who are linked to the era of Rama Rajya?
questions.explanation: Sita gave birth to the twins Kusha and Lava, who were raised in Valmiki's hermitage.

questions.q: In whose hermitage were Rama's twin sons raised and taught the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Kusha and Lava were raised in the ashram of sage Valmiki, who taught them the Ramayana.

questions.q: Rama Rajya is held up in tradition primarily as a model of what?
questions.explanation: Rama Rajya endures as the ideal model of dharmic, righteous governance rather than mere conquest.

questions.q: Rama's faithful devotee who served his court and is celebrated in the era of Rama Rajya was?
questions.explanation: Hanuman, the devoted vanara, remained a beloved servant and devotee of Rama.

questions.q: According to the Uttara Kanda, the doubts of a common washerman concerned which person?
questions.explanation: A washerman's gossip questioning Sita's purity led Rama to send her to the forest, a famous episode of the reign.

questions.q: How long is Rama's reign traditionally said to have lasted, according to the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Valmiki's Ramayana states that Rama ruled for eleven thousand years.

questions.q: In the Uttara Kanda, Rama upheld dharma by acting in the case of a Brahmin's son who died because of whose improper penance?
questions.explanation: The Shambuka episode describes Rama addressing an ascetic's penance said to violate the order of the age, restoring the boy to life.

questions.q: At the close of his reign, by what means did Rama finally depart the world?
options: He vanished from his throne room
questions.explanation: Rama ended his earthly reign by entering the waters of the Sarayu river, returning to his divine form as Vishnu.

questions.q: Which messenger of the gods, whose visit Rama agreed to keep private, set in motion the end of his reign?
options: Time (Kala) in the guise of an ascetic
questions.explanation: Kala (Time), disguised as an ascetic, came on a secret errand, and the broken privacy led to events ending Rama's reign.

questions.q: After Rama's departure, his sons Kusha and Lava were given rule over what?
questions.explanation: Rama established his sons as kings of separate cities, Kusha at Kushavati and Lava at Sravasti.

questions.q: Which brother of Rama disappeared (gave up his life) before Rama's own departure, owing to a vow about an interrupted private meeting?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana gave up his life after interrupting Rama's secret meeting with Kala, honoring Rama's dire vow.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, who is identified as the divine being incarnated as Rama whose reign Rama Rajya represents?
questions.explanation: Rama is regarded as an avatar of Vishnu, and his reign embodies divine order upon the earth.
